{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/networkx/networkx/issues/6722","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/networkx/networkx","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/networkx/networkx/issues/6722/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/networkx/networkx/issues/6722/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/networkx/networkx/issues/6722/events","html_url":"https://github.com/networkx/networkx/issues/6722","id":1734493793,"node_id":"I_kwDOAA2WCc5nYkZh","number":6722,"title":"Inefficient implementation of astar_path","user":{"login":"axtavt","id":2076950,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIwNzY5NTA=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2076950?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt","html_url":"https://github.com/axtavt","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/axtavt/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-05-31T15:31:38Z","updated_at":"2023-05-31T15:31:38Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I noticed that `astar_path()` doesn't give me any noticeable performance advantage over `dijkstra_path()` on my data despite traversing almost two times less nodes.

This happens because `astar_path()` uses `G[node].items()` to iterate over node's neighbors, which causes overhead during iteration:
![astar_orig_profile](https://github.com/networkx/networkx/assets/2076950/8fd5e1ef-cdd2-4725-bb14-a02ff665fc8d)

`dijkstra_path()` uses `G._adj[node].items()`, that doesn't have such overhead. Modifying `astar_path()` to use the same method makes it almost twice faster than before:
![astar_new_profile](https://github.com/networkx/networkx/assets/2076950/323a706e-bb4a-4a10-9d6f-e14b51aeaa93)

### Environment

Python version: 3.9.0
NetworkX version: 3.1
